name: confessional
summary: A small chamber or pair of linked cells where the faithful unburden themselves to a priest. A screen or grille between penitent and confessor. Close, wooden, hushed.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: narrow, often split into two adjacent cells

primary_palette.note: a built-in twin booth — penitent and confessor separated by a screen
split room: a kneeling bench on one side, a chair on the other
a single seat for a priest-in-residence, penitents kneel before
improvised confessional — a hung curtain and a worn kneeler

story_prompts: a confession mid-whisper — a penitent on the kneeler, the confessor still and listening
empty between confessions — the screen clean, a prayer book open on the priest's side
the priest dead inside — a confession that went very wrong, the screen holding something back
a traveler's desperate use — boots muddy on the kneeler, a weapon set aside
a secret meeting — the booth used for something other than confession; chalk signs on the wood

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: dim and close (0.2-0.3) — a single candle per side, or one shared lantern

anti_patterns: keep it small — a confessional is private, not processional
there should always be a boundary between penitent and confessor — screen, curtain, or grille
no altar, no circle — this chamber is about words, not rites
